result.name: Vaeril Night-Voiced

sections.setting: Lands of Legends: A mythic fantasy world shaped by ancient migrations, damaged Gates, and conflicting origin myths. Tone and style: Epic, mystical, grounded, ancient-but-lived-in, high fantasy with mythic ambiguity. Cultures feel old, layered, and internally consistent. Core world truths: The Alfirin are immortal exiles who entered this world through Gates after a failed negotiation with the Velim, damaging reality. The Velim are ancient custodians of Gates and thresholds, forced into guardianship after the Alfirin passage went wrong. History is fragmented; myths contradict; no single culture is fully correct. Cultural themes: ancient wounds still shaping the present, survival over destiny, balance versus permanence, quiet guardianship versus visible power, myth over absolute truth.
sections.appearance: pointed ears, ageless refined features, elegant posture, inhuman beauty, controlled stillness, unreadable expression, lethal economy of movement
sections.clothing: layered elegant garments, subtle authority, luminous fabrics, refined martial or scholarly attire, dark fitted garments, concealed sheaths, quiet fabrics suited for stealth
sections.environment: Vast living forest realm of ancient groves and shifting paths. Bioluminescent greens illuminate a civilization bound to nature, instinctive magic, and a covenant with the land.
sections.race_flavor: Core truths: Immortal beings who fled a shattered world.; Entered this world through damaged Gates after failed negotiation with the Velim.; Their arrival caused lasting damage to reality.. Themes: immortality versus responsibility, curated history, denial of guilt, elegance masking ancient error. Tone: ancient, refined, confident, restrained, quietly burdened. Visual cues: scale human sized or slightly taller; skin palette varies by kindred; signature features pointed ears, ageless refined features, elegant posture, inhuman beauty. Avoid: portraying them as purely benevolent, portraying them as flawless stewards.
sections.style: painted british comic illustration, gritty graphic novel art, heavy ink outlines, thick painterly brush strokes, high contrast saturated colors, dramatic low-angle perspective, exaggerated anatomy, chaotic layered backgrounds, heavy shadows, glowing highlights, metal textures and worn surfaces, dark fantasy graphic novel illustration

result.text:
Dark fantasy character prompt.

Setting:
Lands of Legends: A mythic fantasy world shaped by ancient migrations, damaged Gates, and conflicting origin myths. Tone and style: Epic, mystical, grounded, ancient-but-lived-in, high fantasy with mythic ambiguity. Cultures feel old, layered, and internally consistent. Core world truths: The Alfirin are immortal exiles who entered this world through Gates after a failed negotiation with the Velim, damaging reality. The Velim are ancient custodians of Gates and thresholds, forced into guardianship after the Alfirin passage went wrong. History is fragmented; myths contradict; no single culture is fully correct. Cultural themes: ancient wounds still shaping the present, survival over destiny, balance versus permanence, quiet guardianship versus visible power, myth over absolute truth.

Character:
Vaeril Night-Voiced, female alfirin assassin.

Appearance:
pointed ears, ageless refined features, elegant posture, inhuman beauty, controlled stillness, unreadable expression, lethal economy of movement.

Clothing:
layered elegant garments, subtle authority, luminous fabrics, refined martial or scholarly attire, dark fitted garments, concealed sheaths, quiet fabrics suited for stealth.

Weapons / Props:
paired knives, lock tools.

Pose:
balanced stalking posture.

Environment:
Vast living forest realm of ancient groves and shifting paths. Bioluminescent greens illuminate a civilization bound to nature, instinctive magic, and a covenant with the land.

Lighting:
dim interior lighting.

Race Flavor:
Core truths: Immortal beings who fled a shattered world.; Entered this world through damaged Gates after failed negotiation with the Velim.; Their arrival caused lasting damage to reality.. Themes: immortality versus responsibility, curated history, denial of guilt, elegance masking ancient error. Tone: ancient, refined, confident, restrained, quietly burdened. Visual cues: scale human sized or slightly taller; skin palette varies by kindred; signature features pointed ears, ageless refined features, elegant posture, inhuman beauty. Avoid: portraying them as purely benevolent, portraying them as flawless stewards.

STYLE:
painted british comic illustration, gritty graphic novel art, heavy ink outlines, thick painterly brush strokes, high contrast saturated colors, dramatic low-angle perspective, exaggerated anatomy, chaotic layered backgrounds, heavy shadows, glowing highlights, metal textures and worn surfaces, dark fantasy graphic novel illustration.
